title: Helmia adenocarpa
taxonomy: Helmia adenocarpa is a species within the family Asteraceae and is also classified as belonging to the tribe Anthemideae. The genus Helmia is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. It belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, which is a subclass of the larger group called the dicotyledons. intro: Helmia adenocarpa, also known as Helmholtzia adenocarpa, is a flowering plant species native to the subtropical regions of Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help in relieving respiratory issues, wound healing, and immune system support.
